evidence: Possibly the worst excuse for sci-fi since Plan 9 From Outer Space.
The story isn't linear by any traditional concept, and you have to accept that it gives you questions that it doesn't answer.
I suppose the filmmakers were aiming for a cerebral film, but what they ended up with is enigmatic in the extreme and really goes nowhere.
The story isn't linear by any traditional concept, and you have to accept that it gives you questions that it doesn't answer.
This 2-hour waste of time could've been cut down to 1 hour and still it would've been boring.
The film doesn’t take time to build the tension and the storyline.
The screenplay needed more work, and the editors didn't trim enough.
If you have never seen this film, you would be better served to find a friend or relative who has the DVD's/BluRay's and watch it unedited.
I actually got SO attached to certain characters that I nearly cried when they were no longer there.
The director (who does occasional cameos and shows up in the last scene) put together a marvelous goodbye that sticks with you for a while.
The screenplay seemed to be trying to take the movie through a poorly conceived maze.
The acting is good, the cinematography is decent, but this is an otherwise completely forgettable film.
The father-son dynamic, while not ground-breaking original, is very touching and keeps you intrigued as it evolves through the events and the songs and the stories.

summary: The user shows a strong preference for sci-fi and drama genres, often critiquing slow pacing and narrative complexity. They value high production quality and emotional resonance in films, indicating a desire for engaging and well-crafted storytelling.
